fork.label: Fork
fork.description: Attack two pieces at once with a single move. The opponent can only save one.
fork.tip: Scan every knight and queen square — can it attack two valuable pieces simultaneously?

pin.label: Pin
pin.description: Attack a piece that cannot move without exposing a more valuable piece behind it.
pin.tip: Bishops and rooks create pins along diagonals and files. Look for pieces lined up.

hangingPiece.label: Hanging piece
hangingPiece.description: Capture a piece that has no defender. Free material wins games.
hangingPiece.tip: Before every move, ask: does my opponent have any undefended pieces?

backRankMate.label: Back rank mate
backRankMate.description: Deliver checkmate along the back rank when the king is trapped by its own pawns.
backRankMate.tip: After castling, always ensure your king has an escape square (h3/h6).

skewer.label: Skewer
skewer.description: Like a pin in reverse — attack a high-value piece that must move, exposing a lower-value piece behind it.
skewer.tip: Check if a rook, bishop, or queen can skewer along a file, rank, or diagonal.

discoveredAttack.label: Discovered attack
discoveredAttack.description: Move a piece to reveal an attack by the piece behind it. Two attacks for the price of one.
discoveredAttack.tip: Look for situations where moving one piece unmasks an attack from a different piece.

deflection.label: Deflection
deflection.description: Force a defending piece to move away from its defensive role.
deflection.tip: Identify key defenders. Can you force them away with a sacrifice or forcing move?

mateIn1.label: Mate in 1
mateIn1.description: Deliver checkmate in a single move. Pattern recognition speed.
mateIn1.tip: Always check checks — can any check result in immediate mate?

mateIn2.label: Mate in 2
mateIn2.description: Deliver checkmate in exactly two moves. Your move forces a response, then mate.
mateIn2.tip: Find the forcing move that limits opponent to one response, then checkmate.

sacrifice.label: Sacrifice
sacrifice.description: Give up material temporarily to gain a decisive positional or tactical advantage.
sacrifice.tip: If taking your piece leads to checkmate or winning more material back, sacrifice it.
